title: Clarify Phase 0 folder structure vs GitHub portfolio clone
description: 00-foundations/04-your-workspace.md:113-121 has the reader create ~/guild-projects/portfolio and ~/guild-projects/scratch. Then 00-foundations/05-git-just-enough.md:158-163 has the reader clone guild-portfolio from GitHub into ~/guild-projects/portfolio/, which produces a nested ~/guild-projects/portfolio/guild-portfolio/ — the outer portfolio folder becomes a redundant wrapper. Phase 1 then uses 'cd ~/guild-projects/portfolio/guild-portfolio' as the working directory. A novice will wonder why there are two 'portfolio' folders and whether they put their project in the wrong one. Fix: Either (a) remove the 'mkdir portfolio' step from 04-your-workspace.md so the clone in the next chapter produces ~/guild-projects/guild-portfolio/ with no wrapper, or (b) rename the created folders to make the distinction obvious (workspace vs your-portfolio), or (c) add an explicit explanation in 05-git-just-enough.md about why there is nesting and what each level is for. Option (a) is cleanest.
